title: Big Shot Bob – Shoot Around Ep 126 – Yes He Did
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: In Friday’s Shoot Around Episode 126, Robert Horry, Jenners and Harper open with birthday talk, including a belated shoutout to Shaquille O’Neal and a side discussion about remembering birthdays and leap-day birthdays. They answer a listener question about the AP college basketball top teams (Duke, Arizona, Michigan, Florida, and others), discussing injuries, Florida’s early tough schedule, and why Duke—and Boozer—stand out, plus they promote the station’s bracket challenge. They debate Pat Beverley’s comments about players crying after games and Horry shares the one time he got emotional after a loss. The show also touches on Michael Jordan downplaying the GOAT debate, critiques a wild NBA outfit, discusses the World Baseball Classic and rooting for disliked rivals on Team USA, and closes with “damn, I’m old” moments like nagging injuries, vision changes, and daylight savings time.   00:00 Shootaround Kickoff 00:32 Birthdays And Shaq Shoutout 03:08 College Hoops Top Teams 05:34 Bracket Challenge Plug 06:51 Pat Bev Tears Debate 09:46 Jordan On Goat Debate 12:53 Shea’s wild outfit roast 13:47 NBA suit era rules 16:11 World Baseball Classic fandom 18:21 Damn I’m old moments 21:46 Kids these days conclusion

summary: Mike Facci is joined by Mark Montieth to discuss Ivica Zubac making his Indiana Pacers debut, giving fans their first look at the team’s new center and how he fits into the offense and defense moving forward. title: The Cocaine Quarterback Owed the Cartel $4 Million… How He Made It Out Alive
summary: Former University of Southern California athlete Owen “O-Dog” Hanson lived a double life.  Known as the “Cocaine Quarterback,” Hanson went from popular college athlete to the leader of a massive international criminal operation involving drug trafficking, sports gambling, and money laundering across the U.S., Australia, and Latin America.  At one point, Hanson owed a cartel $4 million, a debt that could have cost him his life.  In this episode of ATS Unplugged, Hanson tells the unbelievable story of how a USC Trojans football walk-on ended up moving millions for a cartel, running an international gambling network, and eventually facing a 21-year federal prison sentence after a joint investigation by the Federal Bureau of Investigation.  From partying with pro athletes and celebrities to running a global trafficking empire, this is the wild true story of how he survived the cartel, the streets, and federal prison. title: Is Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Officially the Best Player in the World?
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander matches Wilt Chamberlain’s 63-year scoring streak with another clutch performance, igniting MVP conversations—could OKC Thunder’s star be the best scorer since Michael Jordan? Is his consistency more valuable than single-game highs from legends like Kobe Bryant or James Harden? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize debate SGA’s historic run, his impact as a two-way player, and whether he has surpassed Nikola Jokic as the best player in the NBA. The Golden State Warriors' slide down the Western Conference standings has fans questioning Steph Curry’s future and the likelihood of a superstar shakeup, with names like LeBron James and Giannis Antetokounmpo swirling in trade rumors. Plus, insight into the Warriors’ postseason outlook, the Celtics vs. Spurs NBA Finals potential, and whether the Lakers can handle Minnesota Timberwolves’ paint dominance. Can roster risks turn the Warriors into contenders, or is their dynasty finally over? 00:00 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander’s MVP Case & Wilt Record 10:10 — Warriors’ Future & Offseason Star Hunting 24:03 — Celtics vs. Spurs Preview & Lakers-Timberwolves Matchup Everydayer ClubIf you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 251 – Pimpin Ain’t Easy
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Episode 251 of the Big Shot Bob Podcast, hosted by Robert Horry with Brandon Harper and Rob Jenners, opens with the NBA canceling the Atlanta Hawks’ planned “Magic City Night” promotion after Adam Silver cited stakeholder concerns, while noting pre-orders for the hoodie will still be fulfilled and may become valuable memorabilia. The crew then talks NBA storylines including Shai Gilgeous-Alexander’s game-winning shot versus Denver and his 126-game 20+ point streak tying Wilt Chamberlain, OKC’s low-turnover execution, and the Spurs’ dominance and team basketball led by Wembanyama and their guards, plus a brief look at standings. They debate Steve Kerr’s call to shorten the season, react to Jayson Tatum’s return from an Achilles injury, discuss whether the Lakers are better without LeBron, share a Cal State Bakersfield assistant coach/pimping arrest story, highlight a Roswell High homecoming crown moment as Big Shot of the Week, and finish with a Guinness-based “That’s Gotta Be a Record” game.   00:00 Podcast Intro and Free Agency 00:47 Magic City Night Canceled 05:48 NBA Morals and Gambling Debate 07:49 SGA Dagger and Wilt Streak 11:35 OKC Team Ball and Turnovers 15:05 Spurs Statement Win and Title Talk 20:03 How to Defend the Spurs 22:40 Standings Check and Clippers Rise 24:06 Steve Kerr Wants Fewer Games 30:02 Tatum Returns to the Celtics 33:55 Celtics Adjusting Lineup 35:49 Lakers Without LeBron 40:36 Why LeBron Still Matters 43:03 Wild Pimping Coach Story 46:06 Big Shot Homecoming Crown 48:07 That’s Gotta Be a Record Game 55:49 Picking A Record To Break

title: Shakur Stevenson on Being Best Boxer in the World, Teofimo Fight, Floyd & Bud Crawford Mentorship
summary: Shakur Stevenson joins 7PM in Brooklyn for a wide-ranging conversation about boxing, discipline, legacy, and why he believes nobody in the sport is on his level. He talks about his win over Teofimo Lopez at Madison Square Garden, whether a fight with Gervonta “Tank” Davis will ever happen, the rivalry with Devin Haney, and how he sees himself in the current boxing landscape. title: Breezy Johnson’s Unforgettable Olympic Week PLUS Unrivaled, SheBelieves, and College Vibes
podcast_name: A Touch More with Sue Bird & Megan Rapinoe
summary: Unrivaled’s second season has come to an end and Sue had a front row for the semis at Barclays. Megan and Sue talk about the end of the season awards and Stewie’s snub from the first and second teams. With the SheBelieves Cup underway, Megan breaks down what she’s liking about the US lineup so far, and what they need to work on.  Plus, it’s officially March, and the college basketball vibes are already full of madness. Sue ticks through the biggest upsets so far, and reminisces about her senior night at UConn. And we’re not done talking about the Winter Olympics: Breezy Johnson, who just won the gold in women’s downhill skiing, joins us to talk about her time-defying run and why she always dreamed of getting proposed to at the Olympics. Finally, Megan gives a shout out to the NWSL Players Restitution Fund and urges players to consider applying. The application and an FAQ are available on the league’s website here. You can also reach out to them with questions by email at NWSLPlayersfund@bracewell.com or by call/text at: 646-799-2238. title: Pacers Crushed by Clippers 130-107 | Mathurin & IJax Reunion Game
podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: Visit https://prizepicks.onelink.me/LME0/PACE and use code PACE and get $50 in lineups when you play your first $5 lineup! The Pacers fall to the Clippers 130-107, but the final score wasn’t the biggest storyline of the night. For the first time since the Zubac trade, Indiana faced Bennedict Mathurin and Isaiah Jackson — two players the Pacers drafted, developed and ultimately moved at the deadline. Naturally, all eyes were on how the former Pacers performed against their old team. Did Mathurin look like the scorer Clippers fans were hoping for?  Did Isaiah Jackson flash the athleticism Pacers fans remember?  And what did this matchup reveal about the trade from both sides? Alex and Casey Johnson break down the performances from Mathurin and Jackson, what stood out in the reunion game, and what this matchup told us about where both franchises are headed. Plus, the guys discuss Indiana’s struggles in the loss, what went wrong defensively, and the bigger picture implications as the Pacers continue navigating this late-season stretch. title: Derrick Rose’s Enforcer Is Now Ja Morant’s OG
summary: Taj Gibson just signed a two-year deal with the Memphis Grizzlies at 40 years old, and he's pulling up to All The Smoke Unplugged with Matt Barnes to break it all down. From the emotional phone call that brought him back to the NBA, to untold stories from the Derrick Rose-era Chicago Bulls, Taj holds nothing back. He reveals what D-Rose was REALLY like in practice (spoiler: he was NOT quiet), shares wild stories about Joakim Noah, Thibs' no-rules practice wars, and Carlos Boozer's infamous shoe-dye hairline.  Taj also compares Ja Morant to Derrick Rose, breaks down the death of the power forward position, picks his 2028 Olympic big men, and shares what it was like having Wemby grab his shot out of the air. Plus, Taj's childhood friend Tameek Floyd joins to talk about GFB Development and their mission to rebuild the Brooklyn community they grew up in. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 250 – Meat in a Box
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Episode 250 of the Big Shot Bob podcast features Robert Horry, Brandon Harper, and Rob Jenners talking March college basketball, Georgia Tech’s struggles under Damon Stoudamire, and how NIL money and one-and-done recruiting have shifted the sport, including a mention of the Calipari 30 for 30 and the massive combined contracts of his Kentucky players. They debate Jonathan Kuminga’s early production with the Hawks and whether the Warriors’ system limited him, then dive into Atlanta’s Magic City Night promotion, the hoodie release, TI performing, and reactions including Luke Kornet’s request to cancel the event.   The show also hits Lakers issues—JJ Redick and Luka sideline moments, Barkley’s criticism, JJ’s comments about still finding continuity, Deandre Ayton’s role, and LeBron’s future—plus Jason Tatum’s rumored return from an Achilles injury, praise for rookie Kon Knueppel’s three-point pace and Charlotte’s young core, a Big Shot of the Week honoring movers who helped stop an Amber Alert kidnapping, and the “Eat In or Take It Off” strip club vs. restaurant game.   00:00 Episode 250 Kickoff 00:51 March Madness Preview 01:55 Georgia Tech Struggles 03:59 NIL Money Arms Race 05:36 One and Done Era 08:18 Why We Love The Tourney 08:46 Kuminga Sparks Hawks 12:58 Magic City Night Debate 24:06 Lakers Sideline Spat 25:39 Are Lakers Contenders 29:56 JJ Redick Quote Rant 32:06 Pacing Panic in LA 34:59 Identity and Accountability 36:58 LeBron Exit Rumors 40:20 Cap Space and New Owners 41:55 Tatum Achilles Return Debate 47:09 Rookie Sniper Kon Knueppel 52:11 Big Shot Amber Alert Heroes 54:42 Strip Club or Restaurant Game

title: Knicks & Wolves are Better Than You Think + Dream Locations for Top Draft Picks | ALL NBA Podcast
summary: Adam Mares and Jason Timpf break down the biggest NBA games and storylines, starting with the New York Knicks dominating the San Antonio Spurs and what the matchup revealed about both teams. They analyze the Spurs’ halfcourt struggles, the need for more from their ball-handlers, and whether physicality is becoming a weakness. On the Knicks side, they highlight Josh Hart’s big performance, Karl-Anthony Towns’ impact, Mikal Bridges thriving in transition, and OG Anunoby’s defensive versatility, while debating whether New York is defined more by its floor or its ceiling. The episode also covers the Minnesota Timberwolves taking down the Denver Nuggets, Denver’s defensive issues and bench struggles, and concerns around Nikola Jokic’s conditioning and Cam Johnson. They wrap with a look at Julius Randle’s game and a fun draft discussion, including dream destinations for top prospects like AJ Dybantsa, Peterson, and Boozer. title: Club 520 - Jeff Teague says Giannis Antetokounmpo is BETTER than Kevin Garnett: 2nd BEST PF EVER
summary: We’re back with Season 4, Episode 35 of Club 520, where Jeff Teague, DJ Wells, and B Hen debate the best power forwards of all time. The guys all agree that San Antonio Spurs legend Tim Duncan is the best of all time, and Jeff says that Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo is the second best of all time. Jeff says he is BETTER than Kevin Garnett during his peaks with the Minnesota Timberwolves and Boston Celtics, and even compares Giannis to Shaq. Giannis is a multiple MVP winner and an NBA champion, and Jeff has high praise for his former teammate. title: Ali Siddiq Confronts 7PM in Brooklyn Over Katt Williams Clip & Talks Netflix, Independence & Legacy
summary: Ali Siddiq returned to 7PM in Brooklyn to address unfinished business from his last appearance and the fallout from the viral Katt Williams episode.Ali explains why he did not appreciate his interview being led with the Katt clip, how it felt watching the rollout unfold, and responds to some of the comments and insinuations made during Katt’s time on the show.What follows is a real and unfiltered conversation. Ali, Melo, and Kaz speak their peace. They work through the tension in the room, unpack accountability,  and how narratives get shaped in today’s media.Beyond that moment, Ali stands firm on building his career independently. From being one of the most streamed comics in the world to rejecting the traditional machine model, he opens up about Netflix turning down his specials, owning his platform, protecting his integrity, and why legacy matters more than industry validation. title: Los Angeles Lakers, LeBron James and Luka Doncic Hit ROCK BOTTOM As NBA Playoffs Loom
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: Los Angeles Lakers spiral after a stunning loss to the shorthanded Phoenix Suns—are LeBron James and Luka Dončić running out of time to turn the season around? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize break down the Lakers’ leadership woes, defensive lapses, and the growing sense that this star-studded roster lacks cohesion. The crew questions Luka’s impact, weighs the team’s fading clutch performance, and debates if the NBA should start shifting the spotlight from LeBron and Steph Curry to rising stars. The heated Rookie of the Year race takes center stage, with Kon Knueppel's historic shooting for the Charlotte Hornets sparking debate over Cooper Flagg’s frontrunner status. title: Big Shot Bob – Shoot Around Ep 124 – Herbert the Pervert
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Robert Horry and the crew kick off Shoot Around episode 124 with jokes and impressions before jumping into a pop quiz: the only NBA player to win a scoring title, rebounding title, blocks title, Rookie of the Year, MVP, Defensive Player of the Year, an NBA championship, and an Olympic gold medal—revealed to be David Robinson. The conversation shifts to Spurs talk, with Horry pushing back on claims that he hates San Antonio, explaining his take on why he doesn’t have Wemby as his MVP and naming his MVP as “Brown,” while still praising the Spurs, Pop, and the 2005 championship team.   They discuss the Chicago Bears potentially moving about 25 miles south to Hammond, Indiana, while likely keeping the “Chicago Bears” name, comparing it to New York teams playing in New Jersey and noting Indiana’s willingness to fund a new stadium. The group also talks about which franchises should never move, highlighting the St. Louis Cardinals and San Diego Padres, and briefly noting the Braves’ move within the Atlanta area. In NBA rules talk, they react to Paul Pierce’s idea of an LED three-point line that turns on and off during games, calling it too gimmicky and better suited to celebrity or All-Star-style events.   The episode also covers UFL rule changes, including a four-point field goal for kicks over 60 yards and banning punts once a team crosses midfield, with the hosts debating whether the NFL might adopt similar ideas and joking about how it would affect Super Bowl squares. Off the court, they break down MLB Players Association executive director Tony Clark resigning after an internal investigation revealed an improper relationship with his sister-in-law, and they react to Mad Dog Russo’s defense while debating how personal life should intersect with employment consequences. They wrap with viral oddities and childhood stories: a 92-year-old woman with Alzheimer’s climbing a seven-foot nursing home gate in 24 seconds, a 12-year-old in North Carolina driving himself to school with a fictitious plate after missing the bus, and the hosts sharing the dumbest things they did as kids—stealing church money for cookies, kicking a hole through a bedroom door, and a messy food fight that led to “double dip” punishments.   00:00 Friday Cold Open 01:25 Wild Twitter Grab Bag 01:40 NBA Trivia Stumper 03:22 Spurs Fans Clapback 05:38 Bears Moving to Indiana 07:17 Teams That Shouldn’t Move 08:43 LED Three Point Line 10:01 UFL Rule Experiments 12:14 Tony Clark Scandal 15:21 92 Year Old Gate Escape 17:11 Kid Drives To School 19:11 Cookie Money Confession 20:42 Foot Through The Door 22:29 Epic Food Fight Fallout 23:46 Double Dip Whoopings

title: NBA Rule That Threatens the MVP Race & DeAndre Ayton's Lakers Role
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: NBA MVP frontrunners Nikola Jokic and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander could miss out due to the controversial 65-game rule—should the league scrap it? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize tackle whether availability or player impact should decide the most prestigious award and debate potential league pivots like schedule reductions and injury exceptions. The conversation heats up with candid takes on DeAndre Ayton’s role with the Lakers, and what it means to be compared to Clint Capela. Spotlight shifts to potential Defensive Player of the Year candidates as Toronto’s Scottie Barnes earns praise for his versatility—yet faces an uphill battle against perennial shot-blockers like Victor Wembanyama and Rudy Gobert. title: Dr. J: Giving Michael Jordan the GOAT Blueprint & Saving the NBA
summary: Basketball history has had only a handful of true faces of the league and we were lucky to sit down with one of them during NBA All-Star Weekend: Julius “Dr. J” Erving. Matt and Stak talk with Dr. J about everything from his legendary runs at Rucker Park to the high-flying, flashy style of the ABA, plus his heated rivalry with Larry Bird and the Boston Celtics. Along the way, we share plenty of laughs, including the inspiration behind Doc’s iconic afro and a hilarious story about his college car at UMass. From influencing generations of players to becoming Michael Jordan’s favorite player and blueprint on and off the court, Dr. J’s impact is undeniable. Step back in time with us as we sit down with true basketball royalty. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Gold Standard: USA Women Olympians and the WNBA's First Rev Share in History
podcast_name: A Touch More with Sue Bird & Megan Rapinoe
summary: The Winter Olympics are over, and Sue and Megan have all the takes, from  gushing over the US Women Olympians, like Hilary Knight, Laila Edwards, and Alysa Liu, to pitying the US Men’s Ice Hockey team for letting their victory get co-opted by a clown. We’re also talking about the USWNT’s roster heading into the SheBelieves Cup: who’s on the team, who’s not, and why it matters. And while we wait for more updates from the CBA negotiations, we’re celebrating the 2025 WNBA triggering a player rev share for the first time in history. We also have a new “Are You a Megan or Are You a Sue” – it’s pretty specific but we think it says a lot about how you go through the world, so tune in and let us know what you think. Finally, we had a couple of very special voicemails we’re sharing, including one fun question about how we fostered our queer community back in the day. title: $100K Fine Explained: Carlisle Defends Nesmith + Pacers Drop 4th Straight
podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: On this episode of Setting The Pace, Mike Facci is joined by David Cole to unpack a situation that escalated quickly. Visit https://prizepicks.onelink.me/LME0/PACE and use code PACE and get $50 in lineups when you play your first $5 lineup! Rick Carlisle revealed on his weekly 107.5 The Fan radio segment why the Pacers were fined $100,000 for violating the NBA’s Player Participation Policy. Carlisle said the league indicated the team could have medicated Aaron Nesmith, who was ruled out with a hand injury and unable to use it properly, in order to make him available. Those on-air comments prompted a response from an NBA spokesperson defending the league’s investigation and ruling. Pre-game, Carlisle doubled down, telling media he prioritizes player health above everything — and that both he and Nesmith were extremely bothered by the fine. But also said he was done talking about the matter.  Facci and David break down:  The league’s reasoning  Carlisle’s public response  The balance between compliance and player safety  And what this means for Indiana moving forward Plus, the Pacers fall for the fourth straight time, this time to Philadelphia, but not without a major bright spot. Micah Potter delivered a career night, pouring in 23 points and continuing to make the most of his opportunity.  Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. title: Are Victor Wembanyama's San Antonio Spurs NBA TITLE Favorites?
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: The San Antonio Spurs dismantle the Detroit Pistons with Victor Wembanyama’s defensive masterclass—does this signal an NBA Finals preview in the making? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize break down the Spurs’ rising status, exploring how Stephon Castle’s relentless play and the team’s athletic depth make San Antonio more than just “Wemby and friends.” The conversation spotlights why the Pistons’ lack of shooting could be a fatal flaw come playoff time and whether their gritty defense is enough to power a deep run. Debate heats up as the crew dissects major Eastern Conference concerns—are the Boston Celtics’ jump-shot dependency and New York Knicks’ defensive inconsistency flesh wounds or fatal flaws? Analysis extends to the playoff readiness of the Cleveland Cavaliers, Toronto Raptors, and Philadelphia 76ers, plus a preview of Knicks vs. Cavs and the impact of Dejounte Murray’s return for the New Orleans Pelicans. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 249 – Maybe I’m In His Way
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Episode 249 of the Big Shot Bob Podcast opens with Brandon Harper and Rob Jenners welcoming seven-time NBA champion Robert Horry back after a week off, including Rob’s trip to Universal’s Epic Universe in Orlando where he spotted Giannis Antetokounmpo visiting with his family right after All-Star weekend. The crew talks about how stars handle public attention, why it’s tough to approach athletes when they’re with family, and the realities of getting recognized in places like airports and theme parks.   They break down the NBA’s new All-Star Game format, praising the shorter, high-intensity setup and crediting the players—especially Victor Wembanyama and Anthony Edwards—for setting a competitive tone. They also criticize the World team’s lack of energy, debate whether a 1-on-1 or 2-on-2 tournament could work, and agree the dunk contest was the worst in memory, brainstorming fixes like having personalities such as Shaq and Kenny “draft” and scout dunkers nationwide since superstars won’t risk losing.   The episode shifts to Pat Riley’s statue unveiling at Crypto.com Arena and Riley’s push for coaches (and benches) to bring back suits and ties, followed by a harsh look at the Lakers after getting blown out by the Celtics on Riley night and James Worthy’s postgame “no heart” comments. They discuss Boston’s defensive approach, the Lakers’ struggles versus top teams, and criticize the team’s effort and urgency—singling out Luka Doncic’s perceived laziness on defense—while previewing the next matchup and noting the looming impact of the 65-game awards eligibility rule on stars like Embiid and others. They also touch on Lindsay Vonn’s severe leg injuries, Michael Jordan’s NASCAR team winning the Daytona 500 and the viral celebration clip that sparked online speculation, Charles “Ko” seeking to play again via the Alabama Supreme Court, and a Barkley “New York Dicks” Inside the NBA moment.   Standings updates include the Thunder and Spurs separating in the West, Detroit leading the East, and a rough stretch for the Kings and Bulls. Big Shot of the Week goes to Team USA hockey for winning Olympic gold in overtime and honoring the late Johnny Gaudreau by bringing his young children onto the ice for the team photo. The show closes with a mascot-themed game inspired by the Olympics’ mascots, running through real vs. fake team mascots across sports, and ending on Robert Horry’s favorite mascot (Denver’s Rocky) and the story of Rocky surviving a parachuting accident.   00:00 Podcast Return 00:56 Universal Trip 01:28 Giannis Spotted 05:17 Fame And Privacy 09:38 All Star Format 13:39 Dunk Contest Rant 15:29 Fixing The Dunk Contest 20:19 Pat Riley Statue 23:16 Suits On Sidelines 28:29 Lakers Get Smoked 29:29 Celtics Win Ugly 30:36 Boston Defense Breakdown 32:17 Lakers Exposed vs Good Teams 33:37 Calling Out Lakers Laziness 36:19 Playoff Plan for Luka 37:57 Rivalry Pride and Pat Riley Night 38:30 65 Game Rule Chaos 40:08 Is It Still Tatum Team 42:08 Tatum Return and Achilles Caution 43:18 Olympics Injuries and Jordan Viral Clip 47:56 College Hoops and Barkley Audio 49:27 Standings Check and USA Hockey Tribute 54:40 Mascot Trivia Game 01:01:45 Rocky Mascot Wild Story

title: Is Victor Wembanyama's Leading the NBA's Best Team? | Detroit Pistons Struggle
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: Hosted by Nick Angstadt (@NickVanExit, Locked On Mavs) & Camille Davis (@CamilleMonae, Locked On Bucks), this episode kicks off by debating whether the San Antonio Spurs are currently the NBA’s best team and breaks down their impressive win over the Detroit Pistons, highlighting balanced play from Victor Wembanyama, Devin Vassell, De'Aaron Fox, and Stephon Castle. The conversation pivots to Cade Cunningham’s challenging night and explores the Pistons' rotation decisions, especially the limited minutes for Ausar Thompson. The hosts analyze Detroit’s offensive struggles and discuss how Jalen Duren’s energy nearly sparked a comeback.  The Sacramento Kings’ long-awaited victory over the Memphis Grizzlies is celebrated, with James Hamm sharing what snapped the 16-game losing streak. The show also examines the Houston Rockets’ blowout win against the Utah Jazz, spotlighting big performances from Jabari Smith Jr. and Kevin Durant, before humorously questioning the Jazz’s decision to sideline Jusuf Nurkic for the remainder of the season due to nose surgery. title: Contender power rankings, Cade’s MVP case, Celtics/Lakers lessons, Team USA & Boozer vs. Dybantsa with John Fanta
summary: On today’s Kevin O’Connor Show, KOC is joined by NBC broadcaster John Fanta to talk everything NBA. They start with Eastern Conference contender power rankings: who’s the number one team in the East? Could Cade Cunningham really be MVP? Then, they turn to Team USA hockey’s gold-medal win against Canada before John tells the story of his call-up to the NBA on NBC by Mike Tirico. Plus, they discuss if Anthony Edwards is the face of the league, address the troubles in Phoenix & Houston, and take a look at the top prospects in this year’s fiery draft class. title: Wood Harris on The Wire, Above the Rim & Feeling Pressure Acting With Denzel Washington
summary: Wood Harris sits down with Carmelo Anthony and Kazeem Famuyide for a conversation that moves between basketball, acting and the discipline behind both. He breaks down how he really landed Motaw in Above the Rim, why hooping separated him from other actors and what it was like filming The Wire in Baltimore at a time when the city felt exactly like the show. Wood reflects on stepping onto set with Denzel Washington for the first time, feeling the pressure in that moment and why he believes sports is more honest than acting. He talks about Tupac’s presence during Above the Rim, playing Spencer Haywood in Winning Time and the mindset that carried him from the west side of Chicago to a 30-year career. Wood also opens up about being kicked out of NYU, nearly losing everything and the turning point that forced him to bet on himself. This is a rare look at the competitor behind the characters and the perspective that shaped one of the most consistent careers in black cinema. Learn more about your ad choices. title: Haliburton Diagnosed with Shingles, Kobe Brown's Career Night + More on the Zubac Trade with Derek Schultz
podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: Alex Golden is joined by Derek Schultz for an episode that has a little bit of everything. We start with the surprising news that Tyrese Haliburton is dealing with shingles — what this means for his recovery timeline and what fans should realistically expect moving forward. Then we discuss the Pacers dropping three straight games out of the All-Star break. While the losses sting, they’ve also boosted Indiana’s draft lottery positioning. Is losing games better for the team in the long run, or could this decision hurt them later? Despite the loss, Kobe Brown poured in a career-high scoring night. What are we seeing from him lately, and is there more there than initially expected? We also revisit the Ivica Zubac trade — what Indiana gave up, why they made the move, and how we’re feeling about it now with more context. And to close it out… we fire up Tankathon and spin the wheel twice for the final two games to see what fate has in store.  Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. title: Deron Williams | Caught TA slipping 👀, The BEEF with Coach Sloan, Kobe 2008 Olympic Stories & more
summary: We back with Deron Williams for a deep dive into one of the most unique careers in basketball. D-Will reflects on his upbringing and the decision to choose Illinois before breaking down the intensity of NBA workouts and the emotions of draft day. He revisits his prime years hooping in Utah, earning All-Star selections, and battling through the Western Conference Finals. Deron opens up about the memorable Coach Sloan situation and how it shaped his journey. The conversation shifts to his time in Dallas, representing Team USA, and his surprising transition into a boxing match. The episode closes with rapid-fire questions revealing insight, humor, and perspective. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Does Any NBA Team Face More Pressure than the Knicks? + Nuggets Troubles and Adam Silver's Tanking Solutions
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: New York Knicks fall to the Detroit Pistons—again. After their fourth loss to Detroit this season, questions mount: Can Jalen Brunson, Mikal Bridges, and Karl-Anthony Towns lead the Knicks past a physical Pistons squad and fulfill their Finals aspirations? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize break down the Knicks’ roster construction, the impact of recent trades, and whether drastic changes are inevitable if the “dream roster” can’t deliver in a wide-open Eastern Conference. Denver Nuggets continue to slide in the West, raising concerns about Nikola Jokic’s health, team chemistry, and looming luxury tax decisions. The conversation explores defensive ratings, Jamal Murray’s role, and the franchise’s future—will Denver’s trustability in the playoffs waver as uncertainty grows? Plus, the team debates the NBA’s latest anti-tanking proposals, questioning whether rule changes or organizational accountability best protect the league’s competitive integrity. 00:00 Knicks vs. Pistons Problem 10:12 Nuggets Slump & Playoff Outlook 22:16 NBA Anti-Tanking Proposals Everydayer Club  If you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. title: Spurs Stay Hot, Cade Adds To MVP Case & How Can The NBA Stop Tanking?
summary: On Fri.'s No Dunks — live from a hotel room in Austin, Texas — the guys discuss the Spurs extending their win streak to seven, Cade Cunningham making his MVP case against the Knicks, a wild ending to Nuggets-Clippers, and potential rule changes to try and combat tanking next season. title: Hoopers Only: A FAFO/Game Recognize Game Crossover
summary: It’s a crossover episode as Fudd Around and Find Out links up with Game Recognize Game for a full collab with Breanna Stewart and Myles Turner.  The group gets into what “game recognize game” really means, Azzi’s mindset heading into the final stretch of her college career, and what it feels like to win — and repeat — at the highest level. Stewie shares classic Geno and CD stories from her UConn days (including getting sent to run the Gampel stairs mid-practice), plus advice for navigating March and the transition to the pros.  Ashanti talks about moving from playing to broadcasting and why telling women’s basketball stories still matters. Myles breaks down life after a decade in Indiana, why community matters when you get drafted, and how fashion and LEGO became part of his identity off the court. This episode of Locked On NBA Game Night features Rylan Stiles and David Ramil breaking down Cade Cunningham’s dominant 42-point game as the Detroit Pistons defeat the New York Knicks and extend their winning streak. The hosts analyze Jamal Murray’s missed free throw and the Denver Nuggets’ surprising loss to the LA Clippers, highlighting struggles from both Murray and Nikola Jokic. They discuss the Cleveland Cavaliers’ blowout victory over the Brooklyn Nets and examine how James Harden’s arrival has immediately impacted the Cavs’ offense. The show also covers Kevin Durant’s efficient 35-point performance in a Houston Rockets win over the Charlotte Hornets, while keeping tabs on injury updates that could shake up the Western Conference playoff race. title: The Knicks CANNOT Beat Detroit Playing This Way
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks are overwhelmed by the Detroit Pistons yet AGAIN—are Mike Brown’s strategic misfires dooming New York’s playoff hopes? XJ breaks down the Knicks’ inability to counter Detroit’s relentless defensive schemes, questioning why New York continues to play into the Pistons’ hands rather than leveraging their roster versatility and five-shooter lineups. Key moments include brutal three-point shooting discrepancies, Cade Cunningham’s MVP-level ascendance, and the Knicks’ struggle with physicality and predictable offensive sets. Discussion centers on the tactical mismatch between these Eastern Conference rivals, the impact of missing key Pistons players like Jalen Duren and Isaiah Stewart, and the urgent need for the Knicks to adapt stylistically. XJ also explores Jeremy Sohan’s defensive role on Cunningham, the psychological toll of repeated defeats, and what genuine adjustments are required for New York to reclaim momentum. Can the Knicks break the cycle, or have the Pistons exposed a fatal flaw?     Timestamps 0:00 — Knicks Embarrassed AGAIN 1:35 — Knicks' Critical Adjustment Needed 10:33 — FanDuel Ad Break 11:22— Quo Ad Break 12:32 — Knicks, Pistons Shooting Disparity 19:59 — 5-Hour Energy Ad Break 20:57 — The Cade Cunningham Problem         Follow & Subscribe on all Podcast platforms 🎧 https://lockedonpodcasts.com/podcasts/locked-on-knicks/       Everydayer Club   If you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: On today's episode of Setting The Pace, Alex Golden and Mike Facci break down what the loss means for Indiana’s Top-4 odds and why the jump from fourth to second is important. We also dive into the on-court developments:  Jarace Walker’s big game and what it says about his continued growth and confidence  Jay Huff playing meaningful, productive minutes and making his case in the rotation  Taelon Peter finding his three-point shot, showing real progress offensively  And injury updates on Kam Jones and Aaron Nesmith, and how their absences are impacting the rotation and evaluation process Then we zoom out...With reports that the league is preparing to enforce stricter anti-tanking rules beginning in 2027, we discuss what that could mean for teams like Indiana moving forward. title: Gil’s Arena Previews The 2nd Half of The NBA Season
summary: Gil’s Arena Previews The 2nd Half of The NBA Season as Gilbert Arenas & The Gil’s Arena Crew highlight the return of NBA action after NBA All Star Weekend and look ahead to the next few months leading up to NBA Playoffs. They examine the standings in the Eastern & Western Conference and debate which teams can catch the leading OKC Thunder & Detroit Pistons, who needs to hit the panic button and give out their dark horses for teams that can make a deep run come playoff season. They also discuss the current race for NBA MVP and debate if Shai Gilgeous Alexander should be the favorite to win the award in back to back season or if Nikola Jokic can steal back his award. Next, they break down the future for LeBron James & the Los Angeles Lakers as the team approaches a pivotal offseason where they must decide between long term success and keeping the King happy. They debate if LeBron will take a hometown discount to remain in LA and allow the team to potentially re-sign Austin Reaves and go after Giannis Antetokounmpo or if he will continue to push for what he deserves as the greatest player in NBA History. Finally, they address the NBA’s battle with tanking as several teams have made it clear that they would rather lose this season to improve their chances at a top pick in a generational draft class and debate what Adam Silver can do to stop the epidemic hurting the NBA. Please give us a Like and Subscribe!! title: Mad Max UNFILTERED AT All-Star Weekend w/ Craig Hodges, Symba, & Jenna Bandy
summary: Last year's NBA All-Star Weekend gave us one of our most iconic moments: the Vernon Maxwell episode. Not only does it stand as one of our funniest and most-watched episodes of all time, but it also brought Mad Max into the ALL THE SMOKE family as a permanent part of the show. So naturally, we had to run it back with this year's live show at The Lighthouse Campus in Venice.  We caught up with Max to talk about how his life has changed since that episode and what he's been up to lately. We also sat down with Craig Hodges to discuss his record-setting NBA Three-Point Contest wins, Symba to break down his cutting-edge approach to breaking into the rap game, and Jenna Bandy to talk about her experience in the NBA Celebrity Game and how she built her career as a content creator.  This one is packed with laughs, completely unfiltered, in the only way ALL THE SMOKE knows how. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Will The Knicks FINALLY Show Up Against The Pistons? With Locked On Pistons Ku Khahil
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks seek redemption against the NBA-leading Detroit Pistons—can Jalen Brunson and Karl-Anthony Towns finally crack Detroit's relentless defense? Host Gavin Schall is joined by Locked On Pistons Ku Khahil dissect the Knicks' struggles in previous matchups, debating whether Mike Brown's squad can match Detroit's physicality and ironclad intensity when it matters most. Key storylines include Cade Cunningham's evolution as a lead creator, the Pistons' defensive dominance since November, and whether recent Knicks acquisitions like Jose Alvarado and Jeremy Sochan can shift the balance in a potential playoff duel. The episode spotlights critical questions around half-court offense, Asar Thompson's impact, and if New York's roster has another gear for postseason battles. Don’t miss expert matchup analysis and insider perspectives on what the Knicks must do to avoid another Motor City meltdown. title: Gil's Arena Reacts To Harden's Take On NBA Loyalty
summary: Chris Paul Going Out SORRY Shocks Gil's Arena as Gilbert Arenas & The Gil’s Arena Crew react to Chris Paul officially announcing his retirement  after a turbulent final season where he was sent home from the Los Angeles Clippers and discuss if it’s sad to see a future NBA Hall of Famer and living legend like CP3 have his career end on such a sour note. They then react to Kyrie Irving announcing that he will sit out the remainder of the season while he recovers from a torn ACL and debate if this is the right move for the Dallas Mavericks’ superstar as they look to surround Cooper Flagg with a championship roster before giving their take on James Harden’s comments on loyalty in the NBA as the Beard believes there is no such thing as he sits on his 4th team in 5 seasons. Finally, they break down Anthony Edwards’ comments to Jalen Johnson about possibly playing for the Atlanta Hawks and react to Kevin Durant allegedly getting caught in another burner gate as negative commentary on his teammates both past and present leaked from suspicious accounts. Please give us a Like and Subscribe!! Today's Gil's Arena Crew : Gilbert Arenas, Josiah Johnson, Rashad McCants,Brandon Jennings & Nick Young Gil's Arena premieres every Wednesday & Thursday at 11:30am PT / 2:30pm ET. title: Is Kyrie Part of the Mavericks' Future & Are the Thunder Vulnerable?
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: Dallas Mavericks face a pivotal offseason as Kyrie Irving’s season-ending knee surgery throws their future into question. Can Cooper Flagg and Kyrie form a championship core, or is a trade inevitable? Wes Goldberg, Matt Moore, and Haize debate Dallas’ rebuilding strategy and trade scenarios involving a top-10 draft pick. Franz Wagner’s indefinite injury shakes up the Orlando Magic’s playoff hopes, sparking questions about long-term roster changes. Oklahoma City Thunder remain title favorites, but injuries to Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Jalen Williams reveal lingering vulnerabilities. The hosts analyze OKC’s clutch stats, shooting development, and whether their depth can withstand another playoff gauntlet. The show wraps with a spirited “Would You Rather?” segment, spotlighting NBA legends like Allen Iverson, Reggie Miller, Chris Bosh, Tracy McGrady, and Scottie Pippen. summary: Gil’s Arena ERUPTS Over NBA All Star Weekend as Gilbert Arenas & The Gil’s Arena Crew react to the festivities from the NBA’s midseason classic and debate if the league finally got it right with their Team USA vs The World Format. They react to Anthony Edwards taking home the All Star Game MVP and discuss Victor Wembanyama setting the tone as the San Antonio Spurs superstar looked to solidify himself as the future face of the NBA with his attempt to resurrect the All Star Game. Finally, they react to Chris Paul announcing his retirement as the NBA Point God officially called it quits after a turbulent season where he was released from the Los Angeles Clippers and debate if it was sad to see an NBA legend of his caliber go out so sorry. summary: Alex and Facci are back with February Mailbag Pt. 2, and this one is all about big-picture roster decisions. Then, the guys tackle Kawhi’s historic year before talking the Spurs' strong finish before the break with Wemby’s epic performances. Finally, can the Knicks make a deep playoff run with their up and down play?  Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices

title: 1-on-1 w/ Napheesa Collier
summary: Azzi and Ashanti sit down with two-time Olympic gold medalist, WNBA All-Star, and Unrivaled co-founder Napheesa Collier for an episode that covers everything from Valentine’s Day takes to the future of women’s basketball.  Phee talks about the evolution of WNBA All-Star Weekend, what makes Unrivaled different, and how the one-on-one tournament came to life. She shares what it’s really like competing in that format, the recovery between games, and how it feels to watch this season from a different perspective. The conversation also dives into Olympic memories, favorite events to watch, and what sport she’d compete in if it wasn’t basketball. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: Big Shot Bob – Shoot Around Ep 123 – You Not Ready
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Join Robert 'Big Shot Bob' Horry and the crew for episode 123 of the 'Shoot Around,' as they dive into some engaging NBA discussions, personal stories, and audience questions. Robert kicks off the episode by announcing a week-long break as he heads to Orlando for a family vacation at the new Epic Universe park. The show's first major topic centers around the widely rumored NBA expansion cities, Vegas and Seattle, and the crew debates other potential cities deserving of a franchise such as Louisville, St. Louis, and Kansas City. They also discuss the nostalgia and business side of bringing back the Seattle Supersonics name and branding if the team returns to the league.   Shifting gears, the conversation pivots to Denver Nuggets' star Nikola Jokic and his chase for the all-time triple-double record. The panel reflects on Jokic surpassing Oscar Robertson to secure the second position and ponders his ability to catch up to the current leader, Russell Westbrook. The focus also turns to the Chicago Bulls and their recent trades, which have left fans perplexed due to the lack of first-round draft picks received in return. The discussion delves into the value and potential of players like DeMar DeRozan, Zach LaVine, and Lonzo Ball.   Finally, the crew answers a variety of listener questions including the feasibility of G-League players returning to college ball, the challenges of adjusting to professional sports, and the weirdest movies they’ve ever seen. They share thoughts on the NCAA's stance on player eligibility, the differences between college and professional basketball environments, and their bewildering movie experiences. This episode is packed with robust debates, entertaining anecdotes, and insightful NBA analysis, making it a must-watch for basketball enthusiasts.   00:00 Introduction and Programming Note 01:20 NBA Expansion Cities Discussion 04:51 Jokic's Triple-Double Milestone 06:27 Chicago Bulls Trade Analysis 10:14 Listener Questions and Alabama Basketball Controversy 12:09 Debate on College vs. G League Development 14:28 The Impact of NIL on College Sports 17:41 Coaches' Perspectives on Returning Players 19:41 Weirdest Movies We've Ever Seen

title: Why Jeremy Sochan Is Actually a BRILLIANT Move
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks may have just raised their championship ceiling with the Jeremy Sochan bold signing. Will his elite perimeter defense help deliver playoff glory at Madison Square Garden? Host XJ breaks down Sochan’s fit in the Knicks’ stacked rotation, evaluating his ability to lock down stars like Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum, and Cade Cunningham during high-leverage postseason moments. Key insights include the challenges posed by Sochan’s shooting woes, comparisons to Haywood Highsmith and Lonzo Ball as alternative buyout targets, and potential ripple effects on minutes for Josh Hart, OG Anunoby, and emerging talents like Mohamed Diawara. XJ explores how Mike Brown’s tactical offense-defense swaps maximize Sochan’s impact and asks whether this specialist approach is the missing piece for a Knicks NBA Finals run. Engage with in-depth analysis on defensive strategy, buyout market risks, and how every incremental move can shape New York’s title ambitions.     Timestamps 0:00 — Knicks Get Jeremy Sochan 02:53 — Sochan’s Elite Strengths & Role 10:52 — FanDuel Ad Break 11:35 — Indeed Ad Break 13:02 — Join Everydayer Club! 13:40 — Sochan’s Weaknesses & Alternatives 19:41 — Gametime Ad Break 20:43 — Season Outlook & Playoff Impact     Follow & Subscribe on all Podcast platforms 🎧 https://lockedonpodcasts.com/podcasts/locked-on-knicks/     Everydayer Club   If you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. title: Are Cleveland Cavaliers and James Harden Now FAVORITES in the East?
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers surge to 3-0 with James Harden, igniting new championship hopes in a suddenly wide-open Eastern Conference. title: 20 Wild Rap Stories From ALL THE SMOKE That Changed the Game
summary: Over the years, we’ve sat down with hip-hop legends who’ve shared untold stories that shaped the culture forever. This compilation brings together 20 of the most jaw-dropping moments from ALL THE SMOKE that you need to hear.  Stories like Fat Joe helping get Biggie his first concert while navigating tension with Tupac. Snoop Dogg speaking on a near death experience with Big Boy. The D.O.C’s career changing accident and one of hip-hop’s biggest what-ifs. Dame Dash admitting he didn’t think Kanye could rap. NWA being on the verge of a reunion before Eazy-E passed.  These are the kind of stories that remind you how unpredictable the journey really is. That’s what makes ALL THE SMOKE different. Guests don’t hold back. They speak on what really happened, who was really there, and how these moments actually shaped hip-hop history.  This is one of the wildest collections of rap stories we’ve ever put together. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 248 – The Disrespectful Face-Mush
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: In this latest episode of the Big Shot Bob Podcast, hosts Robert Horry, Brandon Harper, and Rob Jenners dive headfirst into an action-packed discussion on the latest happenings in the NBA. The episode kicks off with a talk about a recent intense NBA brawl that saw Isaiah Stewart and several others facing suspensions. The guys share their thoughts on NBA fights, how the league's image has been cleaned up over the years, and the implications for the involved teams, particularly focusing on the Pistons' evolving identity as the 'bad boys' of the modern era. They also discuss the dramatic aftermath and the culture of physical play in today's NBA compared to previous generations.   Next, the crew delves into the NBA trade deadline aftermath, dissecting major trades such as James Harden's move to the Cavaliers and Darius Garland going to the Clippers. They analyze the impact of these trades on both teams in the short and long term. The conversation also covers other notable trades like Jonathan Kuminga's trade to the Hawks and Anthony Davis's surprising move to the Wizards. Amidst the trade-talk, there's speculation about Giannis Antetokounmpo's future and the possibility of him leaving the Bucks. The episode also touches on the Jazz's recent decisions, emphasizing their apparent push for tanking and the broader implications for the team and the league.   The podcast isn't just limited to NBA drama; it also discusses the upcoming NBA All-Star Weekend, announcing participants for the Slam Dunk Contest and the Three-Point Contest. There's excitement about Damian Lillard's return to the three-point contest and the prospects of other sharp shooters. Additionally, the hosts celebrate the Seattle Seahawks' Super Bowl victory while sharing personal anecdotes from their Super Bowl weekend, including Robert Horry's surprising visit to Michael Bay's house. The episode wraps up with a lively game of Shootout, where the hosts test their knowledge on NBA Finals MVPs and teams that have never won a Super Bowl.   00:00 Introduction and Podcast Kickoff 01:22 NBA Brawl Breakdown 08:05 NBA Trade Deadline Aftermath 09:03 Giannis Trade Speculations 10:24 James Harden Trade Analysis 14:57 LeBron James' Future 23:14 Anthony Davis to the Wizards 29:51 Utah Jazz Tanking Strategy 36:32 Shaq's Controversial Comments and Jazz's Tanking Strategy 37:48 All Star Weekend Highlights and Predictions 40:07 NBA Standings and Ketamine Controversy 42:12 Super Bowl Recap and Michael Bay's Party 49:41 Big Shot of the Week: Heroic 13-Year-Old Saves Family 51:52 Shootout Game

title: Is Zubac Top 5 in the East? Center Rankings & Furphy Injury Update
podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: Today, Alex Golden and Mike Facci zoom out and rank every starting center in the Eastern Conference. Who truly anchors a contender? Who’s replaceable? And where does Ivica Zubac stack up among the East’s best bigs? We break it down tier by tier and have the honest conversation about value, fit, and ceiling. We start off the show with unfortunate news. Johnny Furphy will miss the remainder of the season after suffering a torn ACL, a brutal setback for a young player who was just beginning to carve out his place. We talk about what this means for his development, how difficult the mental side of this journey can be, and what it means for Indiana’s depth moving forward   You can read all of my latest articles for Pacers on SI, by following this link here!   If you like the show, please leave us a 5-Star rating and review! title: Michael Vick on His NFL Comeback, Changing the QB Position & Finding Purpose After Football
summary: Michael Vick sits down with the 7PM in Brooklyn crew to reflect on one of the most unique careers in football history. From becoming the first Black quarterback drafted No. 1 overall to redefining how the position is played, Vick explains how the game changed and how he had to evolve with it. title: Big Shot Bob – Shoot Around Ep 122 – I Didn’t Know You Had a Shar Pei Head
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: In this week's episode of the Big Shot Bob Pod, Robert 'Big Shot Bob' Horry and his co-hosts dive into a lively sports discussion. The episode kicks off with a debate on the NBA Rookie of the Year contenders, Cooper Flagg and Kon Knueppel, comparing the fierce competition to historic rookie races like LeBron vs. Carmelo, and Jason Kidd vs. Grant Hill. They lean towards Kon Knueppel due to his impressive performance and team impact, despite both rookies having similar records.   The conversation then shifts to the Charlotte Hornets' surprising season performance. The hosts analyze the unexpected success and contributions from players like Brandon Miller and Kon Knueppel, focusing on how the team has exceeded expectations. They discuss the importance of veteran leadership and the challenges of maintaining consistency and seriousness on a young team. The potential for future growth and the cultural impact of the Hornets becoming a relevant NBA franchise again is evaluated.   The show wraps up with some fun Super Bowl prop bets, ranging from the color of the Gatorade bath to the length of the National Anthem and various game outcomes. The hosts also touch on personal anecdotes about going bald and maintaining appearances as they age. title: The weirdest NBA trade deadline ever: The KOC Show explains everything that did & didn’t happen
summary: On today’s Kevin O’Connor show, we’ve got a trade-deadline special! Kevin O’Connor is joined by Tom Haberstroh to dissect any and all movement in the league before the 3 PM ET buzzer, going through the biggest winners and losers in what may be the weirdest trade deadline in NBA history. First, the pair discuss the Milwaukee Bucks’ decision to retain Giannis Antetokounmpo, and what that means for the landscape of the NBA–and for the future outlook of the Golden State Warriors, especially after the anticlimactic end of the Jonathan Kuminga saga. Were the Warriors the biggest losers of the trade deadline? Will another team arise in the summer to win the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes? Next, they discuss the James Harden trade to the Cleveland Cavaliers, and if this move can bring the city of Cleveland to the Finals once again. Then, they muse on what exactly made this deadline so weird: multiple All-Stars being traded to bad NBA teams. They ask: why are bad teams trading for good players? Why are the Utah Jazz putting their top-8 protected first round pick in jeopardy? Why such a paltry return for Anthony Davis, who was traded for Luka Doncic just last year? Plus, they cover all the bases with the more local trades made, from the Chicago Bulls picking up 9 second round picks to the Boston Celtics acquiring their long-coveted big man in Nikola Vucevic. Plus more questions: why didn’t the Sacramento Kings trade for Ja Morant? Why did the Philadelphia 76ers let go of Jared McCain? All that and much, much more on today’s KOC Show! (0:16) Trade deadline instant reactions (4:33) Giannis not traded by Bucks (10:39) Warriors trade Kuminga to Hawks (25:49) Cavs get James Harden (43:34) Wizards acquire Anthony Davis (48:22) Clippers trade Zubac to Pacers (55:57) Jazz trade for Jaren Jackson Jr. (1:02:18) Ja Morant stays in Memphis (1:07:45) Timberwolves trade recap (01:10:18) What are the Chicago Bulls up to? (01:13:05) Celtics trade Simons for Vucevic (01:19:04) 76ers trade Jared McCain to OKC Subscribe to The Kevin O’Connor Show on the your favorite podcast app: 🎧 Apple Podcasts 🎧 Spotify 🖥️ YouTube 📢 Check out the Yahoo Sports podcast network, yahoosports.tv and Yahoo Sports NBA on YouTube

title: 520 in the Morning - Jeff Teague REACTS to Clippers reportedly TRADING James Harden, Lakers DONE with LeBron
summary: On the Tuesday, February 3 episode of 520 in the Morning, Jeff Teague, DJ Wells, and B Hen react to the Los Angeles Clippers reportedly TRADING James Harden ahead of the NBA trade deadline. The Clippers are working with the Cleveland Cavaliers on a possible Darius Garland swap, and the guys discuss why the Clippers would do this since they’ve been the hottest team in the NBA, and how this would position the Cavs in the east. The Philadelphia 76ers beat the Clippers last night without Harden, and the Houston Rockets beat the Indiana Pacers behind a big night from Sengun. Last, the guys react to the news that the Los Angeles Lakers and LeBron James are headed towards a split in the summer. The Lakers are reportedly ready to move on from LeBron, and he feels the same way. Where will LeBron play in the 2026-27 season? title: TRADE PRIMER: Diving Deep On Giannis Antentukuompo, Jose Alverado, Naji Marshall And More! With Jeremy Cohen
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo’s future hangs in the balance as the New York Knicks face a pivotal trade deadline. Could this be the Knicks’ last, best shot to swap Karl-Anthony Towns and land a generational superstar? Gavin Schall brings on cap expert Jeremy Cohen for an inside look at why the front office may be hesitating, and what the fallout could mean for New York’s NBA Finals hopes. From dissecting the feasibility and ripple effects of a Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster to exploring alternative moves—like targeting Jose Alvarado, Tari Eason, or a stretch-big upgrade—no angle is left unexplored. The discussion ranges from Karl-Anthony Towns’ declining trade value, the challenges of building around Jalen Brunson, and the Knicks’ salary cap chess game, to how the team can maximize its championship window. Bold predictions, insider info, and hard-hitting analysis make this a must-listen for any Knicks fan tracking the NBA trade market. podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade talks are heating up during NBA trade deadline week, with the Miami Heat, Golden State Warriors, New York Knicks, and Minnesota Timberwolves all considered the most-likely landing spots. Cleveland Cavaliers spark intrigue with a three-way deal, acquiring Keon Ellis and Dennis Schroder, prompting debate on whether Ellis is the missing piece for Cleveland’s Eastern Conference ambitions. Insights on the Bulls’ asset strategy, Kings’ buy-low moves, and weekend winners—including Nikola Jokic’s return and the Knicks’ defensive dominance—set the stage for deadline drama. summary: The New York Knicks smash the Los Angeles Lakers for their sixth straight win, fueled by Landry Shamet’s electrifying return and a relentless commitment to pace. Tyler Kolek’s emerging role, potential deadline moves, and the importance of buy-in round out a comprehensive analysis of the New York Knicks’ scorching run. title: Big Shot Bob – Shoot Around Ep 121 – Better Not Trade Me To BYU
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: Big Shot Bob and the team dive deep into various hot topics in sports. Starting with a heated discussion about Clemson’s Dabo Swinney calling out other teams for tampering with his players, Big Shot Bob contends that while Dabo has a point, the NCAA’s lack of rules makes it a lawless environment. The conversation shifts to the adverse effects of the transfer portal on high school athletes' recruitment, emphasizing how the portal's existence has complicated the traditional process of getting into college sports.   The episode also covers Deion Sanders' new rules at Colorado, where he implements monetary penalties for player infractions, igniting a debate on the fairness and feasibility of such fines for college athletes who may not be making significant NIL money. The hosts argue whether it’s a fair method of discipline or if it could potentially push players to transfer out. Additionally, the notion of potential trades for college players surfaces, with the crew discussing the complexities and unlikely feasibility of such trades given the current state of college athletics.   Towards the end, the show explores the longevity of players in the modern NBA, citing how current stars like LeBron James and Stephen Curry continue to excel well into their late 30s and early 40s. The hosts contrast this with older generations, attributing the shift to improved fitness regimes and a more offensively driven game. They wrap up with a light-hearted segment about sentimental items that have stood the test of time, sharing personal stories of cherished belongings, from old clothes to long-lived cars, bringing a humorous and nostalgic close to the episode.   00:00 Introduction and Host Greetings 00:58 Dabo Swinney's Rant on Tampering 01:42 NCAA's Lack of Guardrails 02:32 Accountability in College Sports 03:24 Coaches vs. Players: Double Standards 07:16 Impact of Transfer Portal on High School Recruits 10:18 NIL Deals and Player Fines 14:37 The Future of College Sports: Collective Bargaining and Unionization 15:17 The Concept of Trading College Players 17:04 Aging NBA Stars: Performance and Longevity 19:49 Will We See Another 73-9 Season? 21:15 The Emotional Attachment to Personal Belongings

title: Melvin Gregg: 7M Vine Followers, Snowfall’s Coldest Villain & Platform Shadow Bans
summary: Melvin Gregg's journey from Vine star to Hollywood actor has given creators the blueprint for turning social media fame into a thriving career. Best known for his breakout role as Manboy on Snowfall, Gregg opens up about the financial sacrifices he made while on the show and shares behind-the-scenes stories about working with the legendary John Singleton. He also breaks down the difference between social media fame and acting fame, offers advice for content creators looking to transition into entertainment, and exposes the shady tactics platforms use to manipulate creator reach. Plus, Melvin reveals his unexpected passion for gardening and landscaping. And of course, Stak shoots his shot for an acting role yet again. Your time will come, Stak. title: Diana Flores on Flag Football for Dummies + WNBA Rivalries +  Is UConn Untouchable?
podcast_name: A Touch More with Sue Bird & Megan Rapinoe
summary: Megan and Sue remember Alex Pretti, the ICU nurse who was killed by Customs and Border Patrol agents in Minneapolis, and share their thoughts on how to fight feeling overwhelmed with despair. One place to start is to call your Senators, who are voting this week on whether to continue funding ICE through September. As Sue explains on the show, Sharon McMahon makes this very easy through her website, check it out here. For the rest of the show, Megan and Sue take a look at Trinity Rodman’s record-breaking salary with the Washington Spirit, and the brewing rivalry between Paige Bueckers and Caitlin Clark – and why rivalries are a good thing for the league. Plus, UConn is now the last college women’s basketball team standing undefeated. Does it matter? And Mexico’s flag football star Diana Flores joins us to talk about her new book, Flag Football for Dummies, and what it means for the sport to make its Olympic debut in 2028. Finally, Sue and Megan send their flowers to Marina Mabrey and her “crashout jar” with a montage of their favorite moments of Marina crashing out. And Sue reveals one of her first encounters with Marina during the bubble year. A Touch More: The Podcast is produced in partnership with VOX MEDIA and TOGETHXR. Learn more about your ad choices. title: Center of Attention: The Detroit Pistons - A Podcast Miniseries By Setting The Pace
podcast_name: Setting The Pace: A Pacers Podcast
summary: Alex Golden goes solo to talk about the Detroit Pistons terrific frontcourt of Jalen Duren, Isaiah Stewart and Paul Reed.  Jalen Duren is a RFA this season, but will the Pistons offer him the contract he deserves? If not, could Indy swoop in? Isaiah Stewart is crazy, but he is a high-level impact player. Is he too crazy for the Pacers to send a trade offer for? Lastly, Paul Reed has one more year on his contract, but is very gettable...could he make sense as a backup big for Indy? Now at the end of each discussion, the guys give each team and their center depth a letter grade. A being the best, F being the worst.  We hope you enjoy this miniseries! title: Kawhi Leonard SAVES The Los Angeles Clippers' Season
summary: Gilbert Arenas & The Gil's Arena Crew react to Kawhi & The Clippers ripping off 15 wins in their last 18 games to get their season back on the rails following a dreadful start and break down how Kawhi, James Harden & the rest of the Clippers turned into the dangerous team many picked to lead the NBA's Western Conference. They then react to the Greek Freak going down as Giannis Antetokounmpo will be out for awhile after suffering a calf strain and break down what this injury means for an already depleted Bucks team that was already in jeopardy of losing their franchise corner stone. Next they react to Luka Doncic turning up for his former Dallas Mavericks fans as Slovenian Jesus carried the Los Angeles Lakers to a big win over the Mavs, sparking a discussion on Luka's desire to get revenge on his former team. Finally, they react to Derrick Rose getting his flowers as the Chicago Bulls retired the former MVPs jersey on Saturday Night and discuss D-Rose's impact on basketball in the city of Chicago before kicking things over to the Arena Gridiron Crew to break down everything in the NFL Playoffs leading up to the Super Bowl. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 246 – The Man in the Selfie
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: In this episode of the Big Shot Bob Podcast, hosts Robert Horry, Brandon Harper, and Rob Jenners dive into various engaging topics. The episode kicks off with a hilarious and somewhat revealing conversation about Shaquille O'Neal's past quirky habits during meetings, sparking chuckles and light-hearted banter among the hosts. They then shift gears to discuss the landmark upcoming 250th episode of the podcast and reflect on the show's journey over the past five years, recounting their memorable moments and shared experiences.   As the conversation progresses, the hosts pay tribute to Kobe Bryant on the sixth anniversary of his tragic passing. Robert Horry, who played alongside Kobe, shares heartfelt memories and insights into Kobe's evolution from a young, talented player to a seasoned veteran. They delve into the significant impact Kobe had on basketball and its fans, as well as the enduring legacy he left behind. The discussion also touches on the collective grief within the basketball community and the admiration for Kobe's contributions both on and off the court.   The episode rounds off with an in-depth analysis of recent NBA trade rumors, including potential moves involving Giannis Antetokounmpo and Ja Morant. The hosts debate the strategies different teams might employ as they approach the trade deadline and the effect these trades could have on the league. Listeners are also entertained with a fun game segment, 'Black Accomplishment or White Accomplishment,' where the hosts guess the racial background of historical figures based on their achievements.   00:00 Introduction and Podcast Setup 00:36 Faxing and Funny Anecdotes 02:10 Travel Plans and Weather Woes 04:25 Remembering Kobe Bryant 10:04 Lakers' Performance Analysis 19:43 Trade Rumors and Team Dynamics 28:47 Inside the NBA Discussion 34:54 The Decline of ESPN 39:30 Derrick Rose's Legacy 40:36 Clippers' Surprising Comeback 46:11 Super Bowl Matchup Analysis 51:03 Hilarious Fan Reactions 53:46 Skyscraper Climb Thrills 57:18 Black Accomplishment or White Accomplishment?

title: RUMORS: Are The New York Knicks Really Targeting Jrue Holiday?
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: New York Knicks target Jrue Holiday in a surprising trade scenario that could reshape their roster and championship outlook. Could pairing Holiday with Jalen Brunson elevate the team’s backcourt and attract Giannis Antetokounmpo to Madison Square Garden? The potential swap with the Portland Trail Blazers—including a high-value future first-round pick and Mikal Bridges—raises questions about age, salary cap space, and Holiday’s durability. Gavin Schall analyzes Jrue Holiday’s recent performance, on-ball defense, and playmaking fit with the Knicks while weighing the impact of losing Bridges and the risk of a shrinking title window. Discussion covers the possible fallout of moving Karl-Anthony Towns, whether the Knicks can address their wing depth, and how front office moves reflect their ambitions for a superstar acquisition. Will Leon Rose make the blockbuster trade or stick with the status quo? title: GAME NIGHT: Chicago Bulls STUN Celtics With Buzzer Beater | Knicks And Lakers BOUNCE BACK
podcast_name: Locked On NBA – Daily Podcast On The National Basketball Association
summary: The Chicago Bulls, Boston Celtics, New York Knicks, Philadelphia 76ers, Derrick Rose, Kevin Huerter, Jaylen Brown, Mitchell Robinson, Joel Embiid, Karl Anthony Towns, and Jalen Brunson headline this Locked On NBA Game Night episode, with all the latest analysis on NBA matchups, player performances, and team standings. This episode of Locked On NBA Game Night features hosts Gavin Schall and Andy Kamenetzky breaking down an electrifying fourth quarter in Chicago as the Bulls honor Derrick Rose and stun the Celtics with a Kevin Huerter's buzzer-beater. The hosts analyze the Celtics' surprising struggles from three and Jaylen Brown's standout performance, then shift to the Knicks' gritty win over the Sixers, highlighting Mitchell Robinson's impact in containing Joel Embiid and discussing Karl-Anthony Towns' fit in New York. The show wraps with commentary on Brunson's clutch play and potential narrative shifts for the Knicks, while also offering the Sixers' perspective on their tough rebounding night and comeback effort. title: The Knicks Just Went Full Thibs Mode (And It WORKED)
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks overpower the Philadelphia 76ers with dominant third-quarter rebounding and physical play—are they flashing a championship blueprint reminiscent of last season? Jalen Brunson’s isolation scoring, Mitchell Robinson’s relentless work on the glass, and Josh Hart’s athletic resurgence fuel the Knicks’ victory, while Mikal Bridges’ shooting woes raise lineup questions for head coach Mike Brown. Gavin Schall and XJ break down the Knicks’ tactical shift, Deuce McBride’s (still) undervalued impact, and Landry Shamet’s defensive versatility. The discussion explores the Knicks’ struggles with free throws and attention to detail, OG Anunoby’s interior scoring surge, and how adapting multiple playing styles could be crucial in future playoff series. Can the Knicks maintain their edge and fix late-game lapses before the next big Eastern Conference showdown?     Timestamps 0:00 — Knicks Returning to Form 1:48 — Thibs Ball is Back 3:59 — Versatility & Playoff Implications 6:54 — Lineup Analysis & Key Players 8:30 — Landry Shamet “The New DiVincenzo?” 10:31 — Game Time Ad Break 11:51 — Fourth Quarter Meltdown 16:46 — Closing Small, Cat’s Foul Trouble 22:01— FanDuel Ad Break 23:22 — Deep Dives: OG’s Athleticism & Player Spotlights 25:25 — XJ's Deuce McBride Rant 31:08 — Closing Thoughts & Shoutouts       Follow & Subscribe on all Podcast platforms 🎧 https://lockedonpodcasts.com/podcasts/locked-on-knicks/     Everydayer Club   If you never miss an episode, it’s time to make it official. title: Vic Mensa Unfiltered: Chicago, ICE, Kanye & the Lies We’re Fed
summary: Nobody is using their platform to educate, challenge, and inspire quite like Vic Mensa. From viral orange-tree monologues to fearless conversations about politics, identity, and culture, Vic joins ALL THE SMOKE for one of our most wide-ranging and thought-provoking interviews yet. Vic breaks down how he uses social media as a tool for activism, the realities of Venezuelan immigration in Chicago, and how redlining and systemic violence continue to shape the city. He reflects on growing up biracial, his powerful ayahuasca experience, and the role music plays in influencing today’s youth. We also dive deep into Chicago’s legendary music scene, including unforgettable stories about coming up with Chance The Rapper, being in the room while Kanye West recorded “30 Hours,” and the moment Vic’s own career began to take off. Plus, he talks acting on The Chi, sheds light on today’s activists making a real difference, and shares what it was like filming Still With Smoke entirely on an iPhone in Senegal. This one is equal parts history lesson, cultural deep dive, and raw personal reflection. See omnystudio.com/listener for privacy information.

title: BREAKTHROUGH: Knicks HUMILIATE Nets, Jalen Brunson and Landry Shamet LEAD Record-Breaking Victory
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks crush the Brooklyn Nets by a historic 54-point margin—is this the reset Knicks fans have been waiting for? Gavin Schall and Kris Pursiainen break down how the Knicks' suffocating defense, fundamental basketball, and full team buy-in powered one of the most dominant wins in franchise history. From Jalen Brunson’s versatile shot creation to Landry Shamet’s flawless shooting and defensive spark, every rotation piece stepped up as the Nets unraveled. Key topics include Mike Brown’s coaching adjustments, the re-emergence of Mitchell Robinson as a paint enforcer, and the depth provided by gritty contributors like Deuce McBride and Josh Hart. The hosts spotlight why Landry Shamet’s return transformed the Knicks’ second unit and examine Jordan Clarkson’s diminishing role. With a revenge game against the Philadelphia 76ers looming, can the Knicks sustain this intensity and silence critics? Don’t miss this energized recap and preview of what’s ahead in the Knicks’ season. title: Big Shot Bob – Ep 245 – Clap It Up For Mediocrity
podcast_name: Big Shot Bob Pod with Robert Horry
summary: In this packed episode of the Big Shot Bob Podcast, hosts Robert Horry, Brandon Harper, and Rob Jenners dive deep into the latest NBA news, including the unfortunate torn ACL of Jimmy Butler and its impact on the Golden State Warriors. They also analyze the All-Star Game selections and discuss the peculiar voting and format changes. Light-hearted and insightful, the trio also reminisces about their personal interactions with mentalist entertainers and how sports athletes often push themselves to the limits.   Switching gears, the conversation heats up as the trio covers the NFL playoffs with extensive analysis of the key games, standout performances, and game-changing moments. The dramatic ending of the Rams-Bears game and the surprising resilience of teams like the Seahawks are dissected in detail. The hosts also share their predictions for the Super Bowl and gauge the impact of recent NFL head coaching changes.   The episode wraps up with a vivid discussion on college football’s national championship game and the legacy of the 2001 Miami Hurricanes. They also play a fun trivia game, reflecting on some of the most iconic teams in sports history and their championship wins. With a mix of humor, expert insights, and engaging storytelling, this episode is a must-listen for basketball, football, and sports history enthusiasts alike.   00:00 Introduction and Podcast Setup 00:48 Jimmy Butler's Torn ACL: Impact on the Warriors 09:15 NBA Player Injuries and Their Causes 15:13 All-Star Game Confusion and Selections 26:54 Kevin Durant's Scoring Milestone 31:28 NFL Playoffs Recap 31:53 Rams vs Seahawks: NFC Title Game Preview 32:38 Texans' Struggles and Broncos' Triumph 40:17 Coaching Changes and NFL Updates 41:45 National Championship Game Recap 48:09 Game Time: Who’d They Beat?

title: INSIDER: The REAL Reason The Knicks Are Falling APART
podcast_name: Locked On Knicks - Daily Podcast On The New York Knicks
summary: The New York Knicks hit a crossroads as locker room frustrations spill onto the court. Can Jalen Brunson and Josh Hart spark a fast turnaround, or will Mike Brown’s aggressive coaching style push the team into deeper turmoil? Gavin Schall and Kris Pursiainen break down the Knicks’ shaky vibes following a disastrous stretch, including stark contrasts between Mike Brown’s demanding pace-and-space philosophy and Tom Thibodeau’s methodical, high-floor approach.  The conversation highlights insider insights from practice, the emotional impact of the NBA Cup win, and tells of a team grappling with exhaustion, entitlement, and breakdowns both physical and mental. Tune in for raw quotes from player-only meetings, speculation about potential Knicks trades involving Karl-Anthony Towns, and why this squad might just flip the switch sooner than anyone expects. Will the Knicks rediscover their grit in time to rescue their season? summary: On today's 21st episode of the Center of Attention, Alex Golden looks at the Los Angeles Lakers to discuss the centers they have on their roster: Deandre Ayton, Jaxson Hayes and Maxi Kleber. Could the Pacers dip their toes in the pool once again for Ayton? Would trading for Ayton or waiting till he hits FA make sense for this group? What about Jaxson Hayes? Could the former 8th overall pick be in play for the Pacers? Alex discusses it all. Now at the end of each discussion, the guys give each team and their center depth a letter grade. A being the best, F being the worst. We hope you enjoy this miniseries!
